model_specific_findings.evidence: export_height_range = _resolve_export_height_range(params, heightmap) is called on line ~1919, before the Unity-compat resize block on lines ~1922-1929 that reassigns heightmap. The resize changes the actual pixel values (via indexing), but export_height_range still reflects the pre-resize array's min/max.

findings.evidence: export_height_range = _resolve_export_height_range(params, heightmap) is called on line ~1919, before the Unity-compat resize block on lines ~1922-1929 that reassigns heightmap. The resize changes the actual pixel values (via indexing), but export_height_range still reflects the pre-resize array's min/max.
findings.recommendation: Move the _resolve_export_height_range call (and the raw_bytes export) after the Unity-compat resize block so the range matches the actual data being serialized.
findings.title: Height range resolved before Unity resize, causing mismatch with exported RAW

model_specific_findings.evidence: When height_range_min/height_range_max are present, the function calls _resolve_height_range(params, heightmap) which computes range from the array, not from the provided explicit values. This makes the explicit parameters dead code and causes unexpected normalization when a user specifies an exact range.

findings.evidence: When height_range_min/height_range_max are present, the function calls _resolve_height_range(params, heightmap) which computes range from the array, not from the provided explicit values. This makes the explicit parameters dead code and causes unexpected normalization when a user specifies an exact range.
findings.recommendation: When height_range_min and height_range_max are provided, return them directly (with float conversion) instead of delegating to _resolve_height_range which recomputes from the array.
findings.title: _resolve_export_height_range ignores explicit min/max values and always recomputes from heightmap

model_specific_findings.evidence: The old code explicitly normalized heights to [0,1] before export. The new code passes raw heights directly to _export_heightmap_raw, relying on value_range being non-None for normalization. If _resolve_export_height_range returns None (no tiled_world, no use_global_height_range, no height_range params), the function _export_heightmap_raw uses local min/max but the data was never pre-normalized—this works but changes the contract: the old path always produced [0,1] data, now it depends on params.

findings.evidence: The old code explicitly normalized heights to [0,1] before export. The new code passes raw heights directly to _export_heightmap_raw, relying on value_range being non-None for normalization. If _resolve_export_height_range returns None (no tiled_world, no use_global_height_range, no height_range params), the function _export_heightmap_raw uses local min/max but the data was never pre-normalized—this works but changes the contract: the old path always produced [0,1] data, now it depends on params.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all callers of handle_export_heightmap provide appropriate range params, or add a default normalization pass when export_height_range is None to preserve backward compatibility.
findings.title: handle_export_heightmap no longer normalizes heights, breaking non-range callers

model_specific_findings.evidence: if rows < 2 or cols < 2: return terrain_origin_x, terrain_origin_y silently collapses all grid coordinates to a single point for degenerate grids. If a caller passes a 1xN or Nx1 grid, every vertex maps to the origin with no error or warning.

findings.evidence: if rows < 2 or cols < 2: return terrain_origin_x, terrain_origin_y silently collapses all grid coordinates to a single point for degenerate grids. If a caller passes a 1xN or Nx1 grid, every vertex maps to the origin with no error or warning.
findings.recommendation: Raise a ValueError for degenerate grids rather than silently returning a single point, or at minimum log a warning so callers can detect the condition.
findings.title: _terrain_grid_to_world_xy returns origin for degenerate grids, masking upstream bugs
